model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With only 256 fixed training steps, the baseline Adam learning rate is conservative. Increasing it should improve early optimization speed and reduce validation loss within the same budget without adding complexity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The higher learning-rate change hurt loss; this keeps the same training loop but switches to AdamW decoupled weight decay, which often improves early-step generalization in fixed-budget regimes with minimal complexity.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Recent runs suggest 2e-3 is too aggressive under the fixed 256-step budget. Reducing to 1e-3 keeps fast early learning while improving stability, a likely better tradeoff for validation loss.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current optimization settings are close to good; increasing initial channel width is a simple capacity bump that can improve representation quality within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"A cosine decay over just 256 steps may be decaying too quickly; disabling the schedule keeps the stronger constant learning signal that often works better in very short fixed-step training.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With only 256 updates, slightly reducing weight decay can improve fit and lower validation loss while retaining AdamW regularization.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"For a very short 256-step budget, lowering AdamW beta2 can make second-moment estimates adapt faster and improve early optimization, which may reduce validation loss.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Recent tweaks moved away from the strongest region. Reverting beta2 and weight decay to the previously better AdamW settings should restore stronger validation loss before trying new directions.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With stronger convolutional features, a slightly wider MLP head can improve class separation at low complexity and may reduce validation loss within the same step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The wider classifier head regressed validation loss; reverting to the smaller head restores the stronger low-budget capacity/optimization balance.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After increasing model width, a slightly lower learning rate may improve optimization stability and final validation loss within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Restore the stronger learning-rate setting, then test a larger batch to reduce gradient noise and potentially improve validation loss under a fixed number of update steps.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The jump to batch size 128 produced a major gain, suggesting the fixed-step regime benefits from lower gradient noise and more examples per update. Testing 256 is the next high-upside step.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With batch size 256 now reducing gradient noise substantially, a higher learning rate may improve progress per step and further reduce validation loss in the fixed-step setting.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Performance continued improving as learning rate increased at batch size 256, so a further modest increase is a high-upside next step for the same fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Revert to the stronger LR setting, then probe a larger effective batch than 256 to further reduce gradient noise while keeping strong update magnitude in the fixed-step regime.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Performance has improved consistently with larger batch sizes in the fixed-step setup; increasing to 512 is the strongest remaining high-upside test.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"At batch size 512, optimization may support a larger step size; increasing LR is a focused high-upside test to improve fixed-step progress further.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Given repeated gains from larger batches under fixed update count, the final high-upside move is another batch-size increase while keeping the now-strong LR/optimizer settings.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
